items.title: No provider or customer behavioral surface — verified by Layer 1 tests
items.precondition: PR #165 remains a dormant pure internal foundation with no route, worker, schedule, agent-registry entry, UI, API, or other production caller.
items.expected: Layer 2 is not applicable until a future slice wires a production caller and owns live database/provider acceptance.
items.evidence: Fresh verification on code head c7eb4212d3daa76201e0c5ea1d8eb4defa536441 with Node 22.23.1: focused A3 Vitest 1/1 file and 138/138 tests passed; normal full npm test 61/61 files and 594/594 tests passed; TypeScript npx tsc --noEmit exited 0; npm run check:conventions -- --strict exited 0 with 0 structural backend violations; git diff --check exited 0 after this evidence-only edit.
items.notes: This N/A item applies only to provider, operator, and customer behavior. The branch's additive migration is tracked separately in T1. The previous artifact incorrectly mixed 22 passing Layer-1 checks with five live database/provider checks, then reported contradictory 21/26 and 22/27 totals. Live Gemini, SerpApi, and Perplexity checks remain outside this dormant slice and become acceptance requirements of the future executor/caller wiring slice. Strict-conventions advisories remain pre-existing and outside PR #165: 1 historical migration over the size ceiling, 20 route files without an inline auth reference, 1 dangerouslySetInnerHTML file, 2 token-read files, 6 backend .raw() integration-test files, and 102 tenant-scope heuristic model entries.

items.title: Apply, roll back, and re-apply the AEO observation migration on PostgreSQL
items.precondition: An isolated PostgreSQL database compatible with the deployment migration runner.
steps: Run the migration up against the isolated PostgreSQL database.
Run the migration down and verify the added table is removed cleanly.
Run the migration up again and verify the table and constraints are restored.
items.expected: The additive migration applies, rolls back, and re-applies successfully on PostgreSQL.
items.evidence: Not run for PR #165. This item is not a pass.
items.notes: The migration is deployed automatically by the normal branch deployment workflow, so its runtime behavior must be represented even though this PR has no provider or customer caller.
items.waiver: Dave waived PostgreSQL results on 2026-07-16 for this PR. This waiver permits merge without this isolated migration run; it does not convert the result to a pass, and deployment monitoring is still required.
